tier_justification: single work-unit, no irreversible/outward surface (a local shared bash lib sourced by Stop hooks), but touches shared logic consumed by 4 hook scripts and an existing test/verify-criterion (run_all.sh) does not by itself cover the malformed-line goal state pre-fix (that's the bug) — standard tier, not tier-0 exempt.

evals.assert: als_count_invocations returns a correct NONZERO count on a transcript containing one malformed JSONL line plus a valid agentic-loop Skill invocation line, using a fixture built independently of the implementer's own test files.
evals.cmd: cd $(mktemp -d) && repo=/Users/harrison/Github/coderails && printf '%s\n' '{"type":"assistant","message":{"content":[{"type":"tool_use","name":"Skill","input":{"skill":"coderails:agentic-loop"}}]}}' > t.jsonl && printf '%s\n' 'THIS LINE IS NOT JSON AT ALL {{{' >> t.jsonl && ( . "$repo/hooks/scripts/lib/loop_state_common.sh"; n=$(als_count_invocations "$PWD/t.jsonl"); [ "$n" = "1" ] && echo PASS || echo "FAIL got=$n" )
evals.negative_control: Same command against the PRE-FIX lib (git show e63d3d1f:hooks/scripts/lib/loop_state_common.sh) must print FAIL got= (empty, coerced to non-1) — proving the check can fail: cd $(mktemp -d) && repo=/Users/harrison/Github/coderails && printf '%s\n' '{"type":"assistant","message":{"content":[{"type":"tool_use","name":"Skill","input":{"skill":"coderails:agentic-loop"}}]}}' > t.jsonl && printf '%s\n' 'THIS LINE IS NOT JSON AT ALL {{{' >> t.jsonl && git -C "$repo" show e63d3d1f:hooks/scripts/lib/loop_state_common.sh > oldlib.sh && ( . ./oldlib.sh; n=$(als_count_invocations "$PWD/t.jsonl"); [ "$n" = "1" ] && echo UNEXPECTED_PASS || echo "EXPECTED_FAIL got=$n" )
evals.evidence: PASS (n=1, one stderr line skipped_malformed=1). Negative control against pre-fix lib (e63d3d1f): EXPECTED_FAIL got=[] (empty, coerces to 0) — confirms the check can fail on unfixed code.

evals.assert: als_extract_last_text still extracts the final assistant text block when a malformed JSONL line sits between valid entries, using a fixture built independently of the implementer's own test files.
evals.cmd: cd $(mktemp -d) && repo=/Users/harrison/Github/coderails && jq -cn '{type:"assistant",message:{content:[{type:"text",text:"first"}]}}' > t.jsonl && printf '%s\n' 'GARBAGE NOT JSON' >> t.jsonl && jq -cn '{type:"assistant",message:{content:[{type:"text",text:"LOOP-STOP: complete - independent-fixture check"}]}}' >> t.jsonl && ( . "$repo/hooks/scripts/lib/loop_state_common.sh"; out=$(als_extract_last_text "$PWD/t.jsonl" 300); echo "$out" | grep -q 'LOOP-STOP: complete' && echo PASS || echo "FAIL got=[$out]" )
evals.negative_control: Same command against the PRE-FIX lib must print FAIL got=[] (empty) — the whole slurp aborts on the malformed line: cd $(mktemp -d) && repo=/Users/harrison/Github/coderails && jq -cn '{type:"assistant",message:{content:[{type:"text",text:"first"}]}}' > t.jsonl && printf '%s\n' 'GARBAGE NOT JSON' >> t.jsonl && jq -cn '{type:"assistant",message:{content:[{type:"text",text:"LOOP-STOP: complete - independent-fixture check"}]}}' >> t.jsonl && git -C "$repo" show e63d3d1f:hooks/scripts/lib/loop_state_common.sh > oldlib.sh && ( . ./oldlib.sh; out=$(als_extract_last_text "$PWD/t.jsonl" 300); echo "$out" | grep -q 'LOOP-STOP: complete' && echo UNEXPECTED_PASS || echo "EXPECTED_FAIL got=[$out]" )
evals.evidence: PASS got=[LOOP-STOP: complete - independent-fixture check]. Negative control against pre-fix lib (e63d3d1f): EXPECTED_FAIL got=[] (empty) — confirms the check can fail on unfixed code.

evals.assert: loop_state_guard.sh, loop_stall_guard.sh, voice_announce.sh, and unregistered_loop_guard.test.sh's existing malformed-transcript assertions all pass on the merged branch (full hook test suite green, not just the three touched files).
evals.cmd: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails && bash hooks/scripts/tests/run_all.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -qE '^--- run_all: [0-9]+/\1 suites passed ---$' -e '^--- run_all: ([0-9]+)/\1 suites passed ---$' || bash hooks/scripts/tests/run_all.sh 2>&1 | tail -1
evals.negative_control: Introduce a deliberate break (temporarily revert als_count_invocations to `jq -s ...` alone with no tolerant pre-filter) and confirm loop_state_guard.test.sh's malformed-transcript assertions fail: git stash; git checkout e63d3d1f -- hooks/scripts/lib/loop_state_common.sh; bash hooks/scripts/tests/loop_state_guard.test.sh 2>&1 | grep -q '^FAIL' && echo EXPECTED_FAIL; git checkout HEAD -- hooks/scripts/lib/loop_state_common.sh; git stash pop
evals.evidence: bash hooks/scripts/tests/run_all.sh on the rebased worktree branch (fix/loop-state-tolerant-parse @ 5d7da834): '--- run_all: 36/36 suites passed ---' (includes unregistered_loop_guard.test.sh unmodified). Negative control (checked out pre-fix lib on this branch, ran loop_state_guard.test.sh): produced FAIL lines for the malformed-transcript assertions, confirming the check can fail.

evals.assert: als_stable_invocations logs a skipped_malformed=N field on its existing single summary line (not a new line) only when N>0, preserving the zero-lines-when-clean invariant for a fully valid transcript.
evals.cmd: cd $(mktemp -d) && repo=/Users/harrison/Github/coderails && export CLAUDE_DISCIPLINE_LOG="$PWD/disc.log"; export CLAUDE_HOOK_MAX_ATTEMPTS=1; printf '%s\n' '{"type":"assistant","message":{"content":[{"type":"tool_use","name":"Skill","input":{"skill":"coderails:agentic-loop"}}]}}' > clean.jsonl && ( . "$repo/hooks/scripts/lib/loop_state_common.sh"; als_stable_invocations "$PWD/clean.jsonl" >/dev/null ); [ ! -s disc.log ] && echo PASS || echo "FAIL log-not-empty: $(cat disc.log)"
evals.negative_control: Same command with a malformed line appended must produce a NONEMPTY log with skipped_malformed=1: cd $(mktemp -d) && repo=/Users/harrison/Github/coderails && export CLAUDE_DISCIPLINE_LOG="$PWD/disc.log"; export CLAUDE_HOOK_MAX_ATTEMPTS=1; printf '%s\n' '{"type":"assistant","message":{"content":[{"type":"tool_use","name":"Skill","input":{"skill":"coderails:agentic-loop"}}]}}' > t.jsonl && printf '%s\n' 'BAD' >> t.jsonl && ( . "$repo/hooks/scripts/lib/loop_state_common.sh"; als_stable_invocations "$PWD/t.jsonl" >/dev/null ); grep -q 'skipped_malformed=1' disc.log && echo EXPECTED_NONEMPTY || echo UNEXPECTED_EMPTY
evals.evidence: Clean transcript: disc.log stays empty (PASS). Negative control (malformed line appended): disc.log gains exactly 'hook=als_count_invocations reason=none attempts=1 outcome=recovered skipped_malformed=1' — confirms both the invariant and the negative control.
